name: Ants Climbing up a Tree
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
drained and finely chopped (reserve the water)
4 cups Oil, peanut, for deep frying
2 tbsp Cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. Iceberg lettuce leaves, 2 or 3 for each person
2. Bone and finely chop the pork. Do not grind it; you want very small pieces to "climb" on the branches of the noodles.
3. Marinate the pork in soy, wine, and ginger for about 15 minutes.
4. Slice the green onions.
5. Finely chop the cabbage and mushrooms, reserving the soaking water from the mushrooms.
6. Heat oil in a wok until it begins to smoke. Use good ventilation in your kitchen.
7. Open the noodle package and unroll them slightly. Drop into the hot fat in small batches. They will puff up immediately into wonderful white crunchy noodles. Quickly turn to ensure all are cooked. Remove from the pan and drain on paper towels.
8. Heat another wok or frying pan and add 1 tablespoon peanut oil. Add the chopped garlic and toss for a moment.
9. Add the meat and marinade, stirring until the meat is tender but not dry (about 3 minutes on high heat).
10. Remove the meat mixture and add the vegetables to the wok. Stir fry over high heat for 3 more minutes.
11. Return the meat to the pan and add hot bean sauce, sugar, and pepper. Stir for a minute, then add the cornstarch mixed with water. Stir until the sauce thickens.
12. If the sauce is too thin, add a bit of the mushroom soaking water.
13. Add sesame oil and stir.
14. Place the fried noodles on a large platter and pour the meat and vegetable mixture over the noodles.
15. Carefully toss at the table in front of your guests. Each person takes a piece of noodle and meat sauce, places it on a lettuce leaf, and rolls it up like a burrito.
16. Enjoy. Serves 6-8 as part of a Chinese meal.
